name: 贝拉·巴托克
brief: 巴托克出生于匈牙利的纳吉森特米克洛什（今属罗马尼亚），是20世纪最重要的作曲家之一，同时也是民族音乐学的先驱。他与佐尔坦·柯达伊一起深入匈牙利、罗马尼亚、斯洛伐克、土耳其和北非的乡村，用爱迪生蜡筒录音机收集了数千首民歌。二战爆发后他流亡美国，在纽约度过了贫困而孤独的最后五年，1945年因白血病去世。他去世前完成的《第三钢琴协奏曲》是献给妻子迪塔的最后礼物。
briefEn: Bartók was born in Nagyszentmiklós, Hungary (now in Romania), and stands among the most important composers of the twentieth century as well as a pioneer of ethnomusicology. Together with Zoltán Kodály, he ventured deep into the villages of Hungary, Romania, Slovakia, Turkey, and North Africa, recording thousands of folk songs on Edison wax cylinders. After the outbreak of World War II he emigrated to the United States and spent his final five years in New York in poverty and isolation, dying of leukemia in 1945. His Third Piano Concerto, completed just before his death, was a final gift to his wife Ditta.
contribution: 巴托克开创了一条独特的道路——将东欧民间音乐的原始力量与20世纪的先锋技法融为一体。他证明了民间音乐不仅是浪漫主义式的装饰素材，更可以成为重塑音乐语言的根本力量。他发展出一套基于民间音阶和黄金分割比例的独特和声体系，在调性与无调性之间找到了自己的位置。他的六首弦乐四重奏被视为贝多芬之后该体裁最重要的贡献。
contributionEn: Bartók forged a unique path by fusing the raw power of Eastern European folk music with the avant-garde techniques of the twentieth century. He proved that folk material was not merely decorative ornamentation in the Romantic manner but could serve as a fundamental force for reinventing musical language itself. He developed a distinctive harmonic system rooted in folk scales and golden-section proportions, finding his own ground between tonality and atonality. His six string quartets are widely regarded as the most important contribution to the genre since Beethoven.
style: 巴托克的音乐以强悍的节奏驱动力、尖锐的不协和和声和丰富的民间音乐色彩著称。他的节奏借鉴了匈牙利和保加利亚民间音乐中的不规则节拍（如保加利亚节奏），创造出一种粗犷而富于生命力的脉动。他的慢乐章（「夜乐」风格）则展现了另一面——虫鸣鸟叫般的神秘声响，创造出一种大自然的夜间氛围。
styleEn: Bartók's music is defined by ferocious rhythmic drive, biting dissonance, and vivid folk-music coloring. His rhythms draw on the irregular meters of Hungarian and Bulgarian folk music (the so-called "Bulgarian rhythms"), producing a rugged, vital pulse. His slow movements — in the signature "night music" style — reveal another side entirely: mysterious sounds evoking insects and birdsong, conjuring an atmosphere of the natural world after dark.

famousScores.name: 《为弦乐器、打击乐和钢片琴而作的音乐》
famousScores.nameEn: Music for Strings, Percussion and Celesta
famousScores.desc: 20世纪管弦乐文献中最伟大的作品之一
famousScores.descEn: One of the greatest orchestral works of the twentieth century
famousScores.detail:
1936年，巴托克受巴塞尔室内乐团指挥保罗·萨彻之托创作了这部作品，第二年在巴塞尔首演，反响极为热烈。作品采用双弦乐组对置的特殊编制，打击乐和钢片琴居中，形成一种空间立体感。四个乐章构成了一个精密的建筑结构——第一乐章的赋格主题贯穿全曲，在第四乐章以变形的方式再现，体现了巴托克对黄金分割比例的痴迷。

作品第三乐章是著名的「夜乐」典范：钢片琴晶莹剔透的音色与弦乐器的泛音交织，营造出一种大自然深夜的神秘氛围，仿佛蟋蟀、青蛙和夜鸟的合唱。这种效果已成为巴托克最个人化的音乐标签，被无数后来的作曲家模仿。

这部作品在音乐史上的地位极为崇高。它以纯粹的器乐语言，将民间音乐的粗粝力量与严密的复调技法融为一炉，被许多分析家认为是巴托克创作的顶峰，也是20世纪室内管弦乐文献的至高典范。
famousScores.detailEn:
In 1936 Bartók was commissioned by Paul Sacher and the Basel Chamber Orchestra to compose this work; it premiered in Basel the following year to tremendous acclaim. The score calls for a distinctive arrangement of two divided string groups flanking the percussion and celesta at the center, creating a spatial, stereophonic effect. The four movements form a precisely engineered architectural structure — the fugue theme of the first movement permeates the entire work and returns, transformed, in the finale, reflecting Bartók's obsession with golden-section proportions.

The third movement is perhaps the most celebrated example of Bartók's "night music" idiom: the celesta's crystalline tones interweave with string harmonics to conjure the mysterious atmosphere of deep night in the natural world — a chorus of crickets, frogs, and nocturnal birds. This sonic fingerprint became the most recognizable hallmark of Bartók's style and has been imitated by countless composers since.

The work occupies a uniquely exalted position in music history. Through purely instrumental means it fuses the raw energy of folk music with rigorous polyphonic technique, and is regarded by many analysts as the summit of Bartók's art — the supreme achievement in the literature for chamber orchestra in the twentieth century.
famousScores.canonicalPerformer: Béla Bartók, Chicago Symphony Orchestra, Sir Georg Solti
famousScores.canonicalAlbum: Béla Bartók: Music For Strings, Percussion & Celesta
famousScores.canonicalTrackTitle: Music for Strings, Percussion & Celesta, Sz. 106: I. Andante tranquillo

famousScores.name: 《管弦乐协奏曲》
famousScores.desc: 流亡美国期间的代表作，也是他最常被演奏的管弦乐作品
famousScores.descEn: His signature work from the American exile years and his most frequently performed orchestral piece
famousScores.detail:
1943年夏，身患白血病的巴托克在科德角养病。波士顿交响乐团指挥库谢维茨基委托他创作一部大型管弦乐作品，并预付了一千美元稿酬。病痛中的巴托克以惊人的创作热情完成了这部作品，同年12月在波士顿首演，大获成功——这是他生前最后一次真正的成功。「协奏曲」之名源于作品中各个乐器声部轮流以「独奏」方式被突出展示，如同乐手们在竞奏。

作品第四乐章「中断的间奏曲」中，巴托克引用了肖斯塔科维奇《第七交响曲「列宁格勒」》的进行曲主题，并以滑稽的模仿方式将其讽刺变形——这被许多学者解读为他对苏联政治宣传式音乐的讽刺，也是那个政治动荡年代两位大作曲家之间隐秘的跨时空对话。

《管弦乐协奏曲》是20世纪最受欢迎的管弦乐作品之一。它将民间音乐的活力与学院派的精密融为一体，五个乐章各有鲜明个性，既有豪放的节奏游戏，又有深情的挽歌，技巧与音乐性的平衡令人叹服。
famousScores.detailEn:
In the summer of 1943, Bartók was recuperating on Cape Cod, gravely ill with leukemia. Serge Koussevitzky, conductor of the Boston Symphony Orchestra, commissioned him to write a major orchestral work and advanced him a thousand dollars. Despite his failing health, Bartók composed with astonishing energy and completed the piece; it premiered in Boston that December to great success — his last genuine triumph during his lifetime. The title "Concerto for Orchestra" reflects how individual instrument sections are spotlighted in turn as soloists, as if in friendly competition.

In the fourth movement, the "Interrupted Intermezzo," Bartók quotes the march theme from Shostakovich's Seventh Symphony ("Leningrad") and subjects it to comical, mocking distortion — widely interpreted as a satirical jab at Soviet propaganda music, a clandestine dialogue across time and politics between two great composers of a turbulent era.

The Concerto for Orchestra is among the most beloved orchestral works of the twentieth century. It fuses the vitality of folk music with academic precision; each of its five movements has a vivid character of its own, ranging from exuberant rhythmic play to deeply felt elegy, achieving a rare balance of virtuosity and musical substance.
famousScores.canonicalPerformer: Béla Bartók, Chicago Symphony Orchestra, Sir Georg Solti
famousScores.canonicalAlbum: Bartók: Concerto For Orchestra; Dance Suite
famousScores.canonicalTrackTitle: Concerto for Orchestra, Sz. 116: I. Introduction. Andante non troppo – Allegro vivace

famousScores.name: 六首弦乐四重奏
famousScores.desc: 贝多芬之后最重要的弦乐四重奏系列
famousScores.descEn: The most important cycle of string quartets since Beethoven
famousScores.detail:
巴托克的六首弦乐四重奏创作时间跨越了他的整个创作生涯（1908—1939），是一部真正意义上的创作日记——从第一首的晚期浪漫主义到第六首的内敛哀愁，每一首都忠实地记录了他在那个年代的精神状态。第四首（1928）和第五首（1934）是公认的核心：它们运用了拱形结构（ABCBA），大量使用了巴托克独创的「弦乐噪音」技法，如滑音、击弦、拨弦后弦与木板相撞的声音，将弦乐四重奏的音色可能性推到了极限。

第六首弦乐四重奏创作于1939年，正是巴托克母亲去世、二战爆发、他即将流亡的黑暗时期。四个乐章都以同一段「梅斯托」（沉重的哀歌）主题开头，而这段主题越来越长——如同一个无法摆脱的噩梦，最终在第四乐章占据了整个舞台，成为他离别欧洲的挽歌。

这六首四重奏被誉为自贝多芬晚期四重奏以来该体裁最重要的贡献。它们不仅在技法上将弦乐四重奏推向了新的边界，在精神深度上也达到了少有作品能够企及的高度，至今是职业四重奏团目录中不可或缺的核心曲目。
famousScores.detailEn:
Bartók's six string quartets were composed across his entire creative life (1908–1939) and constitute a genuine artistic diary — from the late-Romantic idiom of the First to the restrained sorrow of the Sixth, each quartet faithfully records his inner state during its era. The Fourth (1928) and Fifth (1934) are widely regarded as the core: they employ arch form (ABCBA) and make extensive use of Bartók's distinctive "string noises" — glissandi, col legno, the snap pizzicato where the string rebounds against the fingerboard — pushing the timbral possibilities of the medium to its limits.

The Sixth Quartet was written in 1939, the darkest period of Bartók's life: his mother had just died, war had broken out, and emigration was imminent. Each of its four movements opens with the same "mesto" (heavy lament) theme, and that theme grows longer with each movement — like a recurring nightmare from which there is no escape — until in the finale it takes over entirely, becoming his farewell to Europe.

These six quartets are universally hailed as the most important contribution to the genre since Beethoven's late quartets. They not only extend the technical frontier of the string quartet but achieve a depth of spiritual expression equaled by very few works in any medium, remaining indispensable to every professional quartet ensemble's repertoire.
famousScores.canonicalTrackTitle: String Quartet No. 4, Sz. 91: I. Allegro

famousScores.name: 《小宇宙》 Mikrokosmos
famousScores.desc: 153首钢琴进阶练习曲，20世纪钢琴教学的圣经
famousScores.descEn: 153 progressive piano pieces — the bible of twentieth-century piano pedagogy
famousScores.detail:
《小宇宙》（1926—1939）是巴托克为他的儿子彼得学钢琴而编写的，最初只是一本启蒙教材，最终演变为6册共153首钢琴小品的庞大体系，难度从最简单的五指练习到音乐厅级别的演奏会曲目，构成了一个「小型宇宙」。书名「Mikrokosmos」（希腊语，意为「小宇宙」）正是巴托克对这套作品的精妙定位——它在153首小品中浓缩了他对音乐语言的全部理解。

作品涵盖了大量民间音乐素材（匈牙利、保加利亚、罗马尼亚），同时系统地介绍了不协和和声、双调性、不规则节奏、全音阶等现代技法。第六册中的「保加利亚节奏」系列，用4+2+3、3+2+3等复合拍子模拟了巴尔干民间音乐中复杂的律动，既是绝妙的音乐练习，也是完整的音乐会曲目。

《小宇宙》至今仍是世界上最重要的钢琴教育文献之一。它独特地将教学功能与艺术价值融为一体——每一首小品都是精心打磨的艺术品，而整套系统又完整地展示了20世纪的音乐语汇。钢琴学生通过它学到的不仅是技术，更是一种看待音乐的方式。
famousScores.detailEn:
Bartók began composing Mikrokosmos (1926–1939) for his son Péter's piano lessons. What started as a primer for a child gradually grew into a vast architecture of 153 pieces in six volumes, spanning difficulty from the simplest five-finger exercises to full-scale concert repertoire. The title — Greek for "small world" — perfectly captures Bartók's vision: a microcosm in which his entire understanding of musical language is distilled into 153 miniatures.

The collection incorporates a wealth of folk music sources (Hungarian, Bulgarian, Romanian) while systematically introducing modern techniques: dissonant harmony, bitonality, irregular rhythms, and whole-tone scales. The "Dances in Bulgarian Rhythm" of volume six, employing compound meters such as 4+2+3 and 3+2+3, replicate the complex pulse of Balkan folk music and serve simultaneously as superb musical exercises and self-contained concert pieces.

Mikrokosmos remains one of the most important pedagogical texts in the piano literature. It uniquely fuses teaching function with genuine artistic value — every piece is a carefully crafted miniature, while the whole system offers a complete survey of twentieth-century musical language. Piano students who work through it gain not just technique but an entire way of listening to and thinking about music.
famousScores.canonicalTrackTitle: Mikrokosmos, BB 105, Vol. 1: Nos. 1-6. 6 Unison Melodies

famousScores.name: 《蓝胡子公爵的城堡》
famousScores.desc: 唯一的歌剧，七扇门后隐藏着人类心灵的暗面
famousScores.descEn: His sole opera — behind seven doors lie the dark recesses of the human soul
famousScores.detail:
《蓝胡子公爵的城堡》（1911）是巴托克唯一完成的歌剧，剧本由匈牙利诗人巴拉日·贝拉根据法国民间故事改编。整部歌剧只有两位角色——蓝胡子公爵与他的新婚妻子尤迪特——和七扇神秘的门。尤迪特执意打开一扇又一扇门，每次开门都伴随着一场音乐的启示：武器库、秘密花园、广袤领土……直到第七扇门后，她发现了蓝胡子之前三位妻子的幽灵。

巴托克将匈牙利语言的自然声调完美融入音乐语言，创造了一种只属于匈牙利语的独特宣叙调风格。歌剧1918年首演时，布达佩斯观众对其心理剖析的深度感到震惊——整部作品就是一场关于人类内心最隐秘欲望、权力与爱的哲学探讨。七扇门的结构被许多学者解读为弗洛伊德式的象征。

这部歌剧在演出史上命运多舛——它最初被拒绝出版，首演也推迟了多年。然而今天它被认为是歌剧史上最重要的20世纪作品之一。它的无场景变换（全剧发生在一个封闭的空间）、极度内敛的戏剧张力和匈牙利民族音乐的灵魂，共同创造了一种在歌剧史上独一无二的音乐剧场体验。
famousScores.detailEn:
Bluebeard's Castle (1911) is Bartók's only completed opera, with a libretto by the Hungarian poet Béla Balázs, adapted from the French folktale. The entire opera features only two characters — Duke Bluebeard and his new bride Judith — and seven mysterious locked doors. Judith insists on opening them one by one; each reveals a musical revelation: the armory, the secret garden, the vast domain... until behind the seventh door she discovers the ghosts of Bluebeard's three previous wives.

Bartók perfectly fused the natural cadences of the Hungarian language into his musical idiom, creating a recitative style unique to that tongue. When the opera premiered in Budapest in 1918, audiences were startled by the depth of its psychological exploration — the entire work is a philosophical meditation on the most hidden human desires, power, and love. The seven-door structure has been widely interpreted by scholars as Freudian symbolism.

The opera had a troubled history — it was initially rejected for publication and its premiere was long delayed. Today, however, it is ranked among the most important operatic works of the twentieth century. Its single unchanging setting, extreme dramatic restraint, and the spirit of Hungarian folk music combine to create a music-theater experience unique in the annals of opera.
famousScores.canonicalPerformer: Béla Bartók, Kolos Kováts, Sylvia Sass, Istvan Sztankay, London Philharmonic Orchestra, Sir Georg Solti
famousScores.canonicalAlbum: Solti - Bartók - Compilation by Chicago Symphony Orchestra

famousScores.name: 《第二钢琴协奏曲》
famousScores.desc: 钢琴与管弦乐的搏斗，巴托克最强悍的协奏曲作品
famousScores.descEn: A ferocious contest between piano and orchestra — Bartók's most physically demanding concerto
famousScores.detail:
《第二钢琴协奏曲》（1930—1931）是巴托克三首钢琴协奏曲中技术最艰难、个性最强悍的一首。它由巴托克本人首演，1933年在法兰克福，汉斯·罗斯鲍德指挥。作品第一乐章以无弦乐的管乐队和钢琴展开对话，创造出一种近乎巴洛克时代协奏曲的粗犷质感；第二乐章则是典型的「夜乐」，钢琴在弦乐轻柔的弦音背景上奏出如梦如幻的夜景，之后突入保加利亚节奏的暴力插入。

巴托克创作这部协奏曲时，正处于其艺术生涯最硕果累累的时期，也是他的民族音乐学研究最为密集的阶段。作品中随处可见从保加利亚和土耳其民间音乐中提炼的节奏模式——不对称的节拍单元被组合成复杂的节奏织体，如同大型的音乐拼图。

这部协奏曲以其极高的技术难度著称于钢琴界，被认为是钢琴协奏曲文献中最有挑战性的作品之一。然而它的音乐价值与技术难度相得益彰——宏大的结构规模、原始的节奏力量和深邃的夜色意象，使其成为20世纪协奏曲文献中不可或缺的重要作品。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Piano Concerto No. 2 (1930–31) is the most technically ferocious and individually assertive of Bartók's three piano concertos. The composer himself gave the premiere in Frankfurt in 1933 under Hans Rosbaud. The opening movement sets piano against a wind orchestra with no strings, generating a rugged texture reminiscent of Baroque concerto grosso; the second movement is a characteristic night-music episode — the piano traces dreamlike nocturnal landscapes over quietly shimmering strings, before Bulgarian rhythms erupt with violent force.

Bartók was composing this concerto at the most fertile period of his career, simultaneously at the height of his ethnomusicological research. Rhythmic patterns distilled from Bulgarian and Turkish folk music appear throughout — asymmetrical metric units combined into complex rhythmic fabrics, like an elaborate musical jigsaw puzzle.

The concerto is notorious among pianists for its extreme technical demands and is considered one of the most challenging works in the concerto literature. Yet its musical substance is fully equal to its difficulty — in its vast formal scope, primordial rhythmic energy, and deep nocturnal imagery, it stands as an indispensable landmark of the twentieth-century concerto.
famousScores.canonicalPerformer: Béla Bartók, Géza Anda, Deutsches Symphonie-Orchester Berlin, Ferenc Fricsay
famousScores.canonicalTrackTitle: Piano Concerto No. 2, Sz. 95: I. Allegro

famousScores.name: 《第二小提琴协奏曲》
famousScores.desc: 20世纪最重要的小提琴协奏曲之一，献给小提琴家泽·阿兰伊
famousScores.descEn: One of the most important violin concertos of the twentieth century, dedicated to Zoltán Székely
famousScores.detail:
1937年，匈牙利小提琴家泽·塞克勒委托巴托克创作一首小提琴变奏曲，但巴托克将其扩展为一部完整的三乐章协奏曲（1937—1938）。1939年在阿姆斯特丹由塞克勒首演，随后二战爆发，这部作品沉寂了多年。协奏曲第一乐章有一个秘密：主题其实是12个半音的音列，但巴托克以如此抒情的方式处理，使其完全不像勋伯格式的序列音乐，而更像一首深情的匈牙利民歌。

第二乐章是全曲情感的核心——一系列变奏在弦乐独奏与管弦乐之间展开诗意对话，巴托克在这里将「夜乐」的静谧与巴洛克变奏曲的结构优雅融为一体。第三乐章回到了第一乐章的主题素材，但以更为豪迈的姿态收束，构成完整的戏剧弧线。

《第二小提琴协奏曲》是巴托克独特美学立场的集中体现——他在序列技法与调性传统之间寻求一种个人化的平衡，不盲目跟随勋伯格，也不退守浪漫主义。这部作品与贝多芬、勃拉姆斯和西贝柳斯的协奏曲并列，是20世纪小提琴协奏曲的绝对杰作。
famousScores.detailEn:
In 1937 the Hungarian violinist Zoltán Székely commissioned Bartók to compose a set of variations for violin and orchestra, but Bartók expanded the project into a full three-movement concerto (1937–38). Székely gave the premiere in Amsterdam in 1939; then war broke out and the work fell into silence for years. The first movement harbors a secret: its main theme is in fact a row of all twelve chromatic pitches, yet Bartók treats it with such lyrical warmth that it sounds nothing like Schoenbergian serialism — it sings more like a deeply felt Hungarian folk song.

The second movement is the emotional heart of the concerto — a series of variations unfolds in poetic dialogue between the solo violin and orchestra, where Bartók fuses the stillness of night music with the structural elegance of Baroque variation form. The finale returns to the first movement's thematic material and drives it to a grand, sweeping conclusion, completing the dramatic arc.

The Violin Concerto No. 2 is a concentrated embodiment of Bartók's distinctive aesthetic position — seeking a personal equilibrium between serial technique and tonal tradition, following neither Schoenberg blindly nor retreating into Romanticism. Standing alongside the concertos of Beethoven, Brahms, and Sibelius, it is one of the absolute masterpieces of the twentieth-century violin repertoire.
famousScores.canonicalPerformer: Béla Bartók, Itzhak Perlman, André Previn, London Symphony Orchestra
famousScores.canonicalTrackTitle: Bartók: Violin Concerto No. 2 in B Major, Sz. 112: I. Allegro non troppo

famousScores.name: 《弦乐嬉游曲》
famousScores.desc: 保罗·萨彻委约之作，轻快与深沉并存的弦乐杰作
famousScores.descEn: A Sacher commission — a string masterpiece balancing wit and depth
famousScores.detail:
《弦乐嬉游曲》（1939）是巴托克最后在欧洲完成的重要作品之一，再次由保罗·萨彻委约。巴托克在短短15天内将其写完——这个创作速度对他来说极不寻常。作品写成后不久，他就踏上了流亡美国的不归路。「嬉游曲」（Divertimento）这个体裁名称源自18世纪的轻娱乐音乐传统，但巴托克赋予了它截然不同的内涵。

三个乐章中，第一乐章以巴洛克协奏曲的精神对立独奏组（concertino）与全奏组（ripieno），充满生气与幽默；第二乐章则突然转入深沉的「夜乐」，充满了不安的半音游移和奇异的弦乐音效，仿佛是巴托克对即将到来的战争和流亡命运的预感；第三乐章以活泼的民间舞曲节奏重新唤回欢快气氛，但其中隐隐的忧郁并未消散。

这部作品常被认为是巴托克「欧洲时期」的压轴之作，在轻松的表面下潜藏着深沉的情感暗流。它证明了即使在生命中最沉重的时刻，巴托克依然能以简洁的形式说出深刻的话语，是弦乐管弦乐文献中不可忽视的重要作品。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Divertimento for String Orchestra (1939) was one of the last significant works Bartók completed on European soil, again commissioned by Paul Sacher. Bartók wrote it in an astonishing fifteen days — a speed quite unlike his usual practice. Shortly after finishing it he embarked on his emigration to the United States, never to return. The title "Divertimento" evokes the light entertainment music of the eighteenth century, but Bartók filled the form with very different content.

Across its three movements, the first sets a baroque concerto grosso against divided concertino and ripieno strings, full of energy and wit; the second plunges into a deep night-music episode, filled with uneasy chromatic wandering and eerie string effects that feel like a premonition of the war and exile now bearing down on the composer; the third recalls the lively folk-dance rhythms of the opening, though an undertone of melancholy never entirely lifts.

The Divertimento is often regarded as the capstone of Bartók's European period, concealing deep emotional turbulence beneath a seemingly carefree surface. It demonstrates that even at the heaviest moments of his life Bartók could speak profoundly within compact forms, and it stands as a significant work in the string orchestral literature.
famousScores.canonicalPerformer: Béla Bartók, Hugh Wolff & Saint Paul Chamber Orchestra, Hugh Wolff, Saint Paul Chamber Orchestra
famousScores.canonicalAlbum: Bartók : Divertimento, Romanian Folk Dances & Kodály : Marosszék & Galánta Dances (Apex)
famousScores.canonicalTrackTitle: Bartók: Divertimento, Sz. 113: I. Allegro non troppo

famousScores.name: 《木刻王子》（芭蕾）
famousScores.desc: 巴托克唯一大型芭蕾，管弦乐配器华彩璀璨
famousScores.descEn: Bartók's only full-length ballet, resplendent in orchestral color
famousScores.detail:
《木刻王子》（1914—1917）是巴托克应匈牙利皇家歌剧院之约创作的芭蕾舞剧，剧本由他的挚友巴拉日·贝拉撰写，讲述王子用木偶赢得公主心的童话故事。这部作品1917年5月在布达佩斯首演，指挥是年轻的埃戈托·多纳伊，演出大获成功——这是巴托克生平头一次在祖国赢得真正的公众认可。

《木刻王子》的管弦乐配器规模宏大，运用了大型管弦乐队的全部资源。作品中可以听到来自理查·施特劳斯和德彪西的影响，但巴托克的民间音乐色彩和独特的节奏语言已经清晰可辨。森林场景中铜管乐和打击乐的运用具有一种原始的壮丽，公主独舞的主题则充满了东欧民间音乐的韵味。

这部作品在巴托克作品目录中常被遮蔽于《蓝胡子公爵的城堡》和《神奇的满大人》的光芒之下，但它有其不可替代的价值：它是巴托克在管弦乐语言上最绚丽的展示之一，也是20世纪初匈牙利音乐复兴运动的重要里程碑。今天它更多以管弦乐组曲的形式在音乐会上演出。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Wooden Prince (1914–17) was commissioned by the Hungarian Royal Opera and bears a libretto by Bartók's close friend Béla Balázs — a fairy tale about a prince who wins a princess's heart through a wooden puppet. The premiere in Budapest in May 1917, conducted by Ernő Dohnányi, was a triumphant success — the first time Bartók received genuine public recognition in his own country.

The Wooden Prince calls for a very large orchestra, deploying all its resources to maximum effect. One can hear influences from Richard Strauss and Debussy, but Bartók's folk-music coloring and distinctive rhythmic language are already unmistakable. The forest scenes use brass and percussion with a primordial grandeur, while the princess's solo dance draws on the melodic idiom of Eastern European folk song.

This ballet is often overshadowed in Bartók's catalog by Bluebeard's Castle and The Miraculous Mandarin, yet it has its own irreplaceable value: it is one of the most brilliant displays of Bartók's orchestral language and a significant milestone in the Hungarian musical renaissance of the early twentieth century. Today it is more frequently performed in concert in the form of an orchestral suite.
famousScores.canonicalPerformer: Béla Bartók, Chicago Symphony Orchestra, Pierre Boulez
famousScores.canonicalAlbum: Bartók: The Wooden Prince; Cantata Profana - Compilation by Béla Bartók

famousScores.name: 《罗马尼亚民间舞曲》Sz.56
famousScores.desc: 六首民间舞曲的精粹，从田野录音到音乐会舞台的华丽蜕变
famousScores.descEn: Six folk-dance jewels — a dazzling journey from field recording to the concert stage
famousScores.detail:
《罗马尼亚民间舞曲》（1915年，原版为钢琴独奏，后由作曲家本人改编为小型管弦乐队版）是巴托克民族音乐学田野调查成果的结晶。六首舞曲均来自他在特兰西瓦尼亚（今属罗马尼亚）收集的真实民间旋律，用蜡筒录音机一首一首录制下来，后经巴托克精心编配，赋予了它们新的音乐生命。

六首舞曲风格各异：《棒舞》节奏短促有力，《腰带舞》轻盈跳跃，《踏步舞》粗犷奔放，《号笛舞》（罗马尼亚波尔卡）活泼欢快，《罗马尼亚波尔卡》婉转流动，《急板》则以飞速的小二度旋律收束全曲。短短十分钟内，六种截然不同的东欧民间舞蹈气质轮番登场，构成一幅瑰丽的民间音乐速写册。

这部作品是巴托克作品中最常被演奏、最受欢迎的作品之一。它以简洁易懂的形式完美展示了他的核心理念：真正的民间音乐不是「土气的」装饰品，而是具有强大生命力和艺术潜力的原材料。无数后来的作曲家和编曲家从这部作品中汲取了灵感。
famousScores.detailEn:
Romanian Folk Dances (1915; originally for solo piano, later orchestrated by the composer for small ensemble) represents the finest fruit of Bartók's ethnomusicological fieldwork. All six dances derive from authentic folk melodies he collected in Transylvania (now part of Romania), recorded one by one on a wax cylinder machine, then carefully arranged by Bartók to give them new musical life.

The six dances are each strikingly different in character: the "Stick Dance" is terse and forceful; the "Sash Dance" light and leaping; the "In One Spot" dance rugged and driving; the "Horn Dance" (Romanian polka) bright and spirited; the flowing "Romanian Polka"; and a closing "Allegro" that races to the finish on flying semitone figurations. In barely ten minutes, six entirely different folk-dance personalities from Eastern Europe appear in rapid succession, composing a vivid sketchbook of folk music.

This is among the most frequently performed and best-loved of all Bartók's works. In its accessible, concise form it perfectly embodies his central conviction: genuine folk music is not a quaint decorative material but a raw material of tremendous vitality and artistic potential. Countless subsequent composers and arrangers have drawn inspiration directly from this work.
famousScores.canonicalAlbum: Béla Bartók: Romanian Folk Dances, Sz. 56 - Single by Béla Bartók
famousScores.canonicalTrackTitle: Romanian Folk Dance No 1: Stick Dance

influence: 巴托克的影响遍及全球。他证明了民间音乐可以成为现代音乐革新的引擎，这一理念启发了利盖蒂、卢托斯瓦夫斯基、潘德列茨基等东欧作曲家群体。他的节奏创新影响了梅西安和史蒂夫·赖希。他的《小宇宙》至今仍是钢琴教育的标准教材。在民族音乐学领域，他的田野调查方法论奠定了该学科的科学基础。
influenceEn: Bartók's influence spans the globe. His demonstration that folk music could power modernist innovation inspired an entire generation of Eastern European composers, including Ligeti, Lutosławski, and Penderecki. His rhythmic innovations left their mark on Messiaen and Steve Reich. Mikrokosmos remains a standard text in piano education worldwide. In the field of ethnomusicology, his rigorous fieldwork methodology laid the scientific foundations of the discipline.
